threat_description: Attacker spoofs a legitimate user via stolen credentials/session (credential stuffing/phishing) to access accounts.
mitigation: Enforce MFA; detect and block credential stuffing with rate limits/bot detection; short-lived, rotated session tokens; device binding and re-auth for sensitive actions; session revocation on anomaly.

threat_description: Users can deny having initiated transactions or requests due to insufficient non-repudiation/audit evidence.
mitigation: Maintain tamper-evident, time-synced audit logs capturing user ID, IP/device fingerprint, request IDs; digitally sign critical requests/receipts; show confirmation screens and email receipts.

threat_description: Session hijacking/fixation or missing CSRF protections allow acting as another user.
mitigation: Secure, HttpOnly, SameSite=strict cookies; rotate session IDs on login; enforce CSRF tokens; strict origin/referrer checks; JWT audience/issuer/nonce checks.

threat_description: Server-Side Template Injection (SSTI) or other input tampering leading to code execution.
mitigation: Enable autoescape; never render user-controlled template strings; use Jinja2 sandboxed environment; rigorous input validation/encoding; dependency patching; WAF rules.

threat_description: Insufficient request/response/audit logging enables denial of actions by users without traceability.
mitigation: Structured, immutable logs with user/session IDs and request IDs; time sync; write-once or tamper-evident storage; log integrity monitoring.

threat_description: XSS, verbose errors, or misconfigured caching discloses sensitive data.
mitigation: Strict CSP; output encoding; avoid reflecting user input; redact errors; disable client caching of sensitive data; secure headers; template autoescape.

threat_description: Request floods or slowloris exhaust front-end resources.
mitigation: WAF, rate limiting, connection timeouts, keep-alive and header size limits, autoscaling/CDN, request body size limits.

threat_description: Template or deserialization flaws enable code execution and privilege escalation on the host.
mitigation: Disable dangerous Jinja2 features; no untrusted deserialization; run as non-root with seccomp/AppArmor; container isolation; least OS/file permissions.

threat_description: JWT forgery/replay (weak secrets, alg=none, missing audience/expiry) allows impersonation.
mitigation: Use strong signing (RS256/ES256), verify alg, iss/aud/exp/nbf, jti with replay cache; rotate keys; short TTL; TLS everywhere.

threat_description: Tampering via SQL injection during credential checks or JWT tampering in transit.
mitigation: Parameterize queries via SQLAlchemy; least-privileged DB roles; strict JWT verification; mTLS between services; input validation.

threat_description: Insufficient login/token issuance logging enables denial of actions and hinders forensics.
mitigation: Log auth attempts, token issuance/refresh/revocations; correlate with IP/device; store tamper-evidently; alert on anomalies.

threat_description: User enumeration or token/credential leakage via verbose errors or logs.
mitigation: Generic authentication errors; avoid logging secrets; minimize JWT claims; encrypt secrets at rest; secure config.

threat_description: Brute-force/password spraying and expensive hashing lead to service exhaustion.
mitigation: Rate limits, CAPTCHA/bot detection, exponential backoff, IP reputation; tune Argon2/bcrypt cost and use adaptive throttling.

threat_description: Privilege escalation via missing authorization on admin or token scope confusion.
mitigation: Enforce RBAC/ABAC; validate scopes on every call; default-deny; separate admin surface; security tests for IDOR and scope bypass.

threat_description: Endpoints trust asserted identity (missing JWT verification), allowing impersonation.
mitigation: Verify JWT signature and claims server-side; enforce session binding; check CSRF on state-changing operations.

threat_description: Parameter tampering (amounts, IDs) enables fraudulent transactions or metadata corruption.
mitigation: Server-side validation; enforce object-level auth; idempotency keys; HMAC-signed intents; double-entry checks for funds moves.

threat_description: Insufficient transaction/audit logging lets users repudiate actions.
mitigation: Append-only audit trails with request IDs and cryptographic integrity; secure time source; regular log integrity verification.

threat_description: IDOR/over-broad queries disclose other users’ transactions/cards.
mitigation: Enforce object- and field-level authorization; tenant scoping; parameterized queries; avoid over-fetching; response filtering.

threat_description: Expensive queries/report generation cause CPU/DB exhaustion.
mitigation: Request quotas; pagination; query timeouts/limits; caching; circuit breakers; backpressure.

threat_description: Unsafe eval/deserialization or command execution leads to privilege escalation/RCE.
mitigation: Avoid eval/exec; no untrusted deserialization; sandbox risky libraries; run with least OS privileges; egress filtering.

threat_description: Unauthenticated or mis-scoped upload endpoints allow identity spoofing or cross-tenant writes.
mitigation: Require auth/authorization on upload; use presigned URLs with short TTL and scope; bind uploads to user/session IDs.

threat_description: Path traversal, filename collisions, or malicious file contents tamper with stored data/system.
mitigation: Normalize/whitelist paths; randomize names; AV/malware scanning; block dangerous types; store outside webroot; checksum verification.

threat_description: Lack of upload event logging allows repudiation or inability to trace file origin.
mitigation: Record uploader identity, timestamps, source IP, file hashes and metadata; store in tamper-evident logs.

threat_description: Serving files without authorization or leaking metadata exposes sensitive content.
mitigation: Private storage; enforce authorization on downloads; presigned short-lived URLs; do not inline sensitive files; encrypt at rest.

threat_description: Oversized uploads, zip bombs, or many concurrent uploads exhaust resources.
mitigation: Max size limits; streaming uploads; concurrency caps; content inspection; timeouts; detect compression bombs before extraction.

threat_description: Vulnerable file parsers (e.g., image/PDF processing) enable code execution and privilege escalation.
mitigation: Use patched libraries; isolate processing in sandbox/container with seccomp/AppArmor; drop privileges; limit syscalls and egress.

threat_description: Data tampering via SQL injection or compromised DB credentials.
mitigation: Strict ORM parameterization; least-privileged roles; separate read/write users; change control; DB ACLs.

threat_description: Lack of immutable audit trails allows repudiation of DB changes.
mitigation: Enable pg_audit; append-only audit schema; WAL archiving; cryptographic log sealing and offsite replication.

threat_description: Sensitive data exposure via misconfigurations, backups, or snapshot leaks.
mitigation: Encrypt at rest and in transit; restrict backup access; row-level security; data masking; secrets management.

threat_description: DB resource exhaustion (locks, long-running queries) causing service degradation.
mitigation: Connection pooling; query timeouts; statement time limits; indexes; load shedding; monitoring and auto-kill of runaway queries.

threat_description: Files altered or deleted (ransomware/insider) corrupting content.
mitigation: Immutable/WORM or versioned storage; checksums; restricted service accounts; signed writes; backup/restore testing.

threat_description: No provenance/versioning allows repudiation of file changes.
mitigation: Enable object versioning; maintain write logs with hashes and timestamps; tamper-evident storage.

threat_description: Publicly exposed storage paths or weak ACLs leak uploaded files.
mitigation: Private buckets/paths; deny-by-default ACLs; encrypt at rest; no direct public links; presigned short-lived URLs for access.

threat_description: Storage exhaustion or I/O saturation prevents reads/writes.
mitigation: Quotas; lifecycle policies; monitoring/alerts; separate hot/cold tiers; autoscaling and backpressure.

threat_description: Impersonation of downstream LLM endpoint or caller identity leads to unauthorized access/actions.
mitigation: mTLS with certificate pinning to provider; domain allowlists/DNSSEC; request signing; workload identity for outbound calls; strict inbound authn/z.

threat_description: Prompt injection via user-controlled inputs causes the agent to exfiltrate data or perform unsafe actions.
mitigation: Input isolation and schema validation; content/URL allowlists; retrieval/attachment sanitization; instruction hierarchy and output filters; tool/function allowlists.

threat_description: Lack of prompt/response/tool-use logging enables repudiation and hinders incident response.
mitigation: Structured logs linking user, context, prompts, responses, and tool calls; PII minimization/redaction; tamper-evident storage; retention policies.

threat_description: Sensitive information disclosure in prompts or outputs, including system prompt leakage.
mitigation: PII scrubbing/redaction; never include secrets in prompts; encrypt context at rest/in transit; detect and block leakage; mask system prompts; retrieval access controls.

threat_description: Unbounded LLM calls or large contexts cause cost blowups or service exhaustion.
mitigation: Per-user/org quotas; rate limits; circuit breakers; response caching; max tokens/context; upstream budget caps and alerts.

threat_description: Excessive agency or insecure tool plugins allow escalation to privileged actions (confused-deputy).
mitigation: Capability sandboxing; explicit allowlists; human-in-the-loop for high-risk actions; per-tool scopes/guardrails; egress and secret access controls.

threat_description: Spoofed provider endpoint or SDK/update compromise leads to adversary-controlled responses.
mitigation: Use official SDKs with signature verification; pin TLS certificates; verify domains; monitor SBOM/attestations; restrict outbound to allowlisted hosts.

threat_description: Provider denies generating a harmful output or request receipt due to lack of traceability.
mitigation: Capture request IDs, timestamps, hashes of prompts/responses; enable provider-side logging/correlation; contractual SLAs for auditability.

threat_description: Request tampering/MITM or client-side manipulation of parameters and CSRF leading to unauthorized actions.
mitigation: TLS 1.3 + HSTS; CSRF tokens; server-side validation/authorization; avoid sensitive data in URLs; integrity/checksums for critical intents.

threat_description: Sniffing or leakage of credentials/PII via insecure transport, referer headers, or browser cache.
mitigation: Enforce TLS/HSTS; secure headers (Referrer-Policy=no-referrer, Cache-Control=no-store); avoid PII in query strings; input redaction in logs.

threat_description: Request floods or large bodies overwhelm front-end and upstream services.
mitigation: WAF/rate limits; request body size/time limits; bot detection; CDN shielding; connection throttling/backpressure.

threat_description: Response tampering/MITM or content injection modifies outputs (e.g., malicious JS).
mitigation: TLS/HSTS; strong CSP; subresource integrity; avoid inline scripts; response signing where feasible.

threat_description: Sensitive data exposed in responses or via client/proxy caching and verbose errors.
mitigation: Minimize sensitive fields; redact errors; Cache-Control: no-store; X-Content-Type-Options; avoid exposing internal IDs.

threat_description: Large/streaming responses and downloads saturate client/network, amplifying DoS.
mitigation: Per-response size/time limits; pagination/chunking; compression and rate limits; abort slow clients.

threat_description: Tampering with login/register payloads or MITM on internal hop alters auth decisions.
mitigation: mTLS/service mesh; request signing; strict schema validation; replay protection (nonce/jti).

threat_description: Credentials or PII disclosure over the wire or through logs/metrics.
mitigation: mTLS; minimize/avoid transmitting secrets; redact logs; secure metrics; OAuth best practices.

threat_description: Burst of auth requests exhausts the service on this path.
mitigation: Per-IP/user rate limits at edge; CAPTCHA; circuit breakers; priority queues for authenticated traffic.

threat_description: Token/session tampering or swap in transit (e.g., man-in-the-middle inside cluster).
mitigation: mTLS; Set-Cookie with HttpOnly/Secure/SameSite=strict; sign tokens (RS/ES); bind token to client context where feasible.

threat_description: Token/session disclosure via headers, referrers, or improper storage.
mitigation: Do not place tokens in URLs; HttpOnly cookies; Referrer-Policy: no-referrer; avoid localStorage for secrets; short TTL.

threat_description: Token refresh/issuance bursts overwhelm channels or caches.
mitigation: Throttle token issuance; exponential backoff; cache headers; staggered refresh; circuit breakers.

threat_description: Tampering with DB connection/queries in transit (downgraded TLS, MITM).
mitigation: Enforce TLS to DB with cert validation; restrict network paths; parameterized queries; least-privileged DB user.

threat_description: Disclosure of credentials or password hashes during auth checks.
mitigation: TLS; avoid SELECTing sensitive columns; hash-and-compare within DB function; encrypt columns; protect backups/snapshots.

threat_description: Auth check bursts exhaust DB connections or locks.
mitigation: Connection pooling; rate limit login attempts; statement_timeout; caching of safe-to-cache metadata.

threat_description: Validation responses altered in transit causing incorrect auth results.
mitigation: TLS; response schema validation; check row counts/expected flags; replay protection.

threat_description: Exposure of unnecessary columns in responses (e.g., password hashes, salts).
mitigation: Principle of least data; views/stored procedures; column-level encryption/masking; avoid logging result sets.

threat_description: Slow/large responses tie up threads and block auth pipeline.
mitigation: Query optimization; pagination; timeouts; circuit breaker fallback to ‘temporarily unavailable’.

threat_description: Parameter tampering or JWT misuse in transit leads to unauthorized actions.
mitigation: mTLS; verify JWTs; server-side validation; idempotency keys for state changes; signed requests for critical actions.

threat_description: Leakage of PII or account identifiers via headers, URLs, or logs.
mitigation: TLS; avoid PII in URL; structured logging with redaction; encrypt sensitive request fields end-to-end where feasible.

threat_description: High rate of state-changing actions overwhelms business logic.
mitigation: Per-user/org quotas; rate limiting; asynchronous job offloading; backpressure/circuit breakers.

threat_description: Response tampering in transit could alter balances/transactions shown.
mitigation: TLS; signed data for critical reads; ETag integrity; client-side verification for critical displays if applicable.

threat_description: Over-broad data in responses discloses other users’ info (multi-tenant leakage).
mitigation: Data minimization; object/tenant scoping; response filtering; schema-based serializers with allowlists.

threat_description: Very large responses or server-sent events overwhelm clients and infrastructure.
mitigation: Pagination; max response size and time; streaming with backpressure; per-client rate limits.

threat_description: Tampering with SQL traffic or parameters over the network leads to incorrect writes/reads.
mitigation: TLS to DB; prepared statements; role separation; firewall rules limiting DB access to service IPs.

threat_description: Disclosure of sensitive query parameters or embedded secrets (e.g., in DSNs).
mitigation: Secrets manager for DSNs; TLS; minimize PII in queries; log redaction; encrypt sensitive columns.

threat_description: Query storms or long transactions overload DB via this path.
mitigation: Statement timeouts; connection pool limits; caching; rate limits; circuit breakers/fail fast.

threat_description: Tampered result sets (MITM) or stale replays lead to incorrect business decisions.
mitigation: TLS; response verification (row counts, checksums); reject unexpected schemas; use transaction IDs.

threat_description: Excess or sensitive fields returned leak PII/PCI data to app and logs.
mitigation: Views/whitelists; row/column-level security; data masking; restrict ORM eager loading; log redaction.

threat_description: Huge result sets overwhelm app memory/threads causing cascade failures.
mitigation: LIMIT/pagination; server-side cursors; memory limits; streaming with backpressure.

threat_description: Tampering with upload metadata (e.g., content-type, filename) changes downstream handling.
mitigation: Strict schema; sanitize filenames; sign metadata (HMAC) with request; enforce server-detected MIME types.

threat_description: Exposure of sensitive metadata (filenames, paths, user IDs) during upload initiation.
mitigation: TLS; minimize metadata; avoid embedding PII in names; log redaction; encrypt sensitive metadata fields in transit and at rest.

threat_description: Oversized or too many concurrent upload requests overwhelm the handler.
mitigation: Max body size; streaming; concurrency caps; per-user quotas; pre-signed URL expirations.

threat_description: Status/metadata tampering (e.g., false success) misleads business logic.
mitigation: mTLS; signed callbacks with shared keys; correlate with server-side computed hashes and sizes; idempotency tokens.

threat_description: Metadata responses leak sensitive attributes (owner IDs, internal paths).
mitigation: Minimize response fields; mask IDs; access checks before returning metadata; log redaction.

threat_description: Burst of callback/status messages overloads business logic message handlers.
mitigation: Rate limiting; queueing with backpressure; collapse duplicate callbacks; circuit breakers.

element: File Upload Handler -> Local File Storage
threat_description: Tampering of binary data or headers in transit to storage (e.g., NFS/S3 MITM).
mitigation: TLS to object store; server-side encryption with customer-managed keys; checksum/ETag verification; signed URLs with short TTL.

element: File Upload Handler -> Local File Storage
threat_description: Exposure of file contents or keys during upload (e.g., misconfigured bucket, sniffing).
mitigation: Private buckets/shares; TLS; server-side encryption; tight IAM; disable public ACLs; VPC endpoints.

element: File Upload Handler -> Local File Storage
threat_description: High-rate or large uploads saturate storage bandwidth/IO.
mitigation: Per-tenant quotas; bandwidth throttling; multipart/chunked uploads with limits; lifecycle policies to free space.

element: Local File Storage -> File Upload Handler
threat_description: Tampered confirmations (e.g., fake ETag) or replays mislead about write success.
mitigation: TLS; verify ETag/checksum vs. local hash; ensure strong consistency or read-after-write checks; nonce-based replay protection.

element: Local File Storage -> File Upload Handler
threat_description: Path, bucket, or internal IDs exposed in confirmations/logs leak structure.
mitigation: Return opaque IDs; avoid logging full paths; encrypt sensitive metadata; strict IAM to prevent listing.

element: Local File Storage -> File Upload Handler
threat_description: Delayed or streaming confirmations hold connections open and exhaust handler threads.
mitigation: Async callbacks; timeouts; bounded thread pools; bulk confirmations.

threat_description: Tampering with metadata inserts or parameters in transit to DB.
mitigation: mTLS; prepared statements; input normalization; least-privilege DB role for writes.

threat_description: Sensitive metadata (PII, file hashes) exposed during insert or in logs.
mitigation: TLS; encrypt sensitive columns; minimize stored metadata; redaction; access controls on logs/backups.

threat_description: High-volume insert traffic saturates DB write capacity.
mitigation: Batching; async queues; write quotas; partitioning/indexing; circuit breakers on spikes.

threat_description: Tampered insert confirmations cause inconsistent application state.
mitigation: TLS; verify row counts/IDs; idempotent operations; compare echoed values with request.

threat_description: Disclosure of internal keys/paths or unrelated rows in confirmation payloads.
mitigation: Views limiting columns; DTO/serializer allowlists; masking; logging hygiene.

threat_description: Slow confirmations/backpressure cause upload handler thread starvation.
mitigation: Timeouts; async processing; bounded queues; fallback retries with jitter.

threat_description: Prompt injection rides along user context to alter downstream agent instructions/actions.
mitigation: Isolate untrusted content; instruction hierarchy; input schema and content filters; tool/function allowlists; strip/escape model-affecting tokens in content.

threat_description: Sensitive data or system prompts included in context leak to the agent and potentially to the model/provider.
mitigation: Redact/minimize context; never include secrets; role-based retrieval; mask/rotate system prompts; encrypt sensitive context at rest/in transit.

threat_description: Huge contexts or high request rate overwhelm the agent pipeline and budgets.
mitigation: Max tokens/context; per-user quotas; circuit breakers; deduplication/caching; admission control.

threat_description: Response tampering or tool-use injection embedded in model outputs leads to unsafe downstream actions.
mitigation: Treat outputs as untrusted; schema/regex validators; require human-in-the-loop for high-risk actions; allowlist tools; strip control tokens.

threat_description: AI responses may contain sensitive data or system instructions that leak when logged or shown.
mitigation: PII redaction before logging/display; classify and filter outputs; least-privilege access to logs/telemetry; data retention limits.

threat_description: Large outputs or streaming tokens overwhelm parsers/UI and backend resources.
mitigation: Max tokens in response; streaming with backpressure; timeouts; chunk validation and truncation; rate limits.

threat_description: MITM/tampering of AI requests alters prompts, tools, or parameters across the Internet boundary.
mitigation: TLS 1.3 with cert pinning; DNSSEC/DoH; egress allowlists; request signing and nonce; verify provider hostnames.

threat_description: PII/secrets in prompts exfiltrated to provider via inference API.
mitre_atlas: AML.T0024: Exfiltration via ML Inference API
mitigation: Data minimization/redaction; per-tenant consent and DPA; encrypt prompts in transit; segregate contexts; use provider data controls (no-train flags).

threat_description: Burst or expensive requests (long contexts/high compute) cause DoS/cost blowouts.
mitigation: Token and RPM quotas; budget caps and alerts; exponential backoff with jitter; cache identical prompts; circuit breakers.

threat_description: Tampering/replay of AI responses or upstream gateway injection alters outputs.
mitigation: TLS with pinning; correlate provider request/response IDs; signed webhooks where available; anti-replay nonces; integrity checks on streamed chunks.

threat_description: Responses may include sensitive training data or echo system prompts that leak into logs/UI.
mitigation: Output filters/classifiers; redact before persistence; enforce data handling policies; provider assurances on data handling; guardrails to block prompt/system leakage.

threat_description: Large or malformed streamed responses exhaust agent parsers/threads.
mitigation: Streaming with backpressure; strict parsers and size/time limits; chunk verification; drop/abort on protocol violations; rate limiting.
